novel_features.name: Net balance overview
novel_features.description: See everything you owe and are owed across every friend and group in one net-position view.
novel_features.rationale: Splitwise returns balances separately per group and per friend; no client aggregates them into a single net position.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this instead of N get_groups + get_friends calls when an agent needs the user's overall money position.

novel_features.name: Debt aging
novel_features.description: List who owes you (and whom you owe) sorted by how long the balance has gone unsettled.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining synced expenses with derived balances to compute days since the oldest open expense per relationship — no single API call returns aging.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the task is 'who never pays me back' or chasing stale IOUs.

novel_features.name: Group ledger with running balance
novel_features.description: Every expense in a group, in date order, with a cumulative running balance per member.
novel_features.rationale: Requires replaying synced expenses in order against per-member shares — the API only returns current balances, never the running history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to audit how a group's balances got to where they are, not just the snapshot.

novel_features.name: Spend analytics rollups
novel_features.description: Total shared spend broken down by category, group, or month from your synced history.
novel_features.rationale: Requires summing synced expense amounts bucketed locally; Splitwise has no analytics, no spend-over-time, no per-category rollups.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for any 'how much did we spend on X' question instead of paging the whole expense list.

novel_features.name: Offline expense search
novel_features.description: Full-text search across your entire expense history, comments, and group/friend names — offline.
novel_features.rationale: Requires an FTS5 index over the full synced ledger; the Splitwise app search is weak and there is no bulk export-and-grep.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to find a specific past expense by keyword without paging the API.

novel_features.name: Settle-up plan
novel_features.description: Compute the minimum set of transfers that zeroes out balances in a group, then optionally record the payments.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a min-cash-flow debt-simplification graph over per-member net balances; no Splitwise client emits a transfer plan.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when a group wants the fewest Venmo transfers to get everyone to zero.

novel_features.name: Activity diff
novel_features.description: Show what changed since your last sync — new, edited, and deleted expenses to review.
novel_features.rationale: Requires diffing synced notifications and updated_after expenses against the local store's last-sync cursor.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to reconcile recent account activity before settling or reporting.

novel_features.name: Split calculator
novel_features.description: Build and preview the exact expense split (equal, exact, percentage, or shares) before recording it.
novel_features.rationale: create_expense requires a per-user paid_share/owed_share array that sums exactly to the total; no Splitwise client computes and previews that body for you.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to turn 'I paid $84, split equally with the trip' into a ready-to-record expense without hand-building the share arrays. Add --record to submit it.

novel_features.name: Recurring-expense detector
novel_features.description: Surface repeating charges (rent, utilities, subscriptions) from your synced history and flag a month missing an expected entry.
novel_features.rationale: Requires grouping synced expenses by normalized description and cadence in SQLite; Splitwise has no recurrence concept and no endpoint that returns it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to catch a shared monthly bill nobody remembered to log this cycle.

narrative.headline: Every Splitwise feature, plus an offline SQLite ledger that powers balance, debt-aging, spend analytics, and full-text search no other Splitwise tool has.
narrative.value_prop: splitwise-pp-cli wraps the full Splitwise API — expenses, groups, friends, comments, settle-ups — and keeps a local copy of your whole ledger. That local store powers a net `balances` view, `debts --aged` (who never pays you back), `spend` rollups by category or month, offline `search`, a group `ledger` with running balances, and a `settle-up` plan that minimizes transfers. Fuzzy name resolution means you never paste a numeric ID.
narrative.auth_narrative: Splitwise authenticates with a personal API key used as an HTTP Bearer token. Register an app at https://secure.splitwise.com/apps to get your key, then set SPLITWISE_API_KEY. OAuth 2.0 (authorization-code) is also supported for multi-user apps, but a personal API key is the fastest path for a power-user CLI.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the binary, config path, and verify state without needing credentials.
Pull your groups, friends, expenses, comments, categories, and currencies into the local store.
See your net position across every friend and group at a glance.
Roll up your shared spend by category from synced history.

troubleshoots.fix: Set SPLITWISE_API_KEY to a key from https://secure.splitwise.com/apps, then run splitwise-pp-cli doctor.
Run splitwise-pp-cli sync first — these read the local store, which is empty until synced.
Splitwise has conservative personal-use limits; re-run sync later, or use sync --since 7d for incremental pulls.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for splitwise-pp-cli when a task involves shared expenses, group trips, roommate bills, or settling up — logging an expense, checking who owes whom, rolling up spend by category, finding a past expense, or computing a settle-up plan. It is the right tool when you want offline analytics over a Splitwise account or scriptable expense automation, not a one-off live lookup.

recipes.title: Net position for an agent
recipes.explanation: Returns just the headline numbers an agent needs to report the user's overall money position.

recipes.title: Inspect a group's members and debts (narrow a verbose payload)
recipes.explanation: get-groups returns deeply nested members + balance arrays; --select keeps only the fields you need so an agent doesn't burn context on the full payload.

recipes.title: Find a forgotten expense
recipes.explanation: Full-text search across your synced expense history for a keyword.

recipes.title: Plan the fewest transfers to settle a trip
recipes.explanation: Prints the minimum-transfer settle-up plan; add --record to create the payment expenses.

trigger_phrases: what do I owe on splitwise
how much did we spend on food
